query: SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing SKHY first trading date July 2026 latest news, pricing date, roadshow status, SEC review as of late June 2026. Is July 10 still the expected trading date? Any reports of delay or schedule slippage?
answer:
Research complete. Summary of findings on the SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing (SKHY):

**July 10 is still the expected trading date** — and there are **no reports of delay or slippage** as of July 8, 2026.

Key points:
- **Roadshow status**: The formal marketing/bookbuilding process **launched Monday, July 6, 2026** (Bloomberg), keeping the deal on schedule.
- **SEC review**: Form F-1 filed June 24; F-1/A amendment filed ~June 30 — review advancing normally. Launch of marketing signals registration is far enough along to price.
- **Pricing date**: Final ADR price to be set by bookbuilding just before the debut (~July 8–9); no firm price range or exact pricing time publicly disclosed.
- **The "tentative" caveat**: July 10 has always been labeled "tentative and subject to change" in SK Hynix's SEC filings — this is standard IPO language, not a specific warning.
- **Deal size**: ~$29.65B gross / ~$28B net; 17.79M new shares; 10 ADRs per share; would be the **largest ADR offering ever** (surpassing Alibaba's $21.8B).
- **Risk factor to note**: The underlying Seoul-listed shares (000660.KS) have been volatile heading into the debut — down ~6.1% on July 7 and ~3% on July 8, and ~28.6% off their 52-week high. This is a market-condition consideration but has **not** been reported as affecting the schedule.

Bottom line: The listing is proceeding on track for July 10, with the roadshow already underway and no delay reports.

sources.title: South Korea's biggest chipmaker SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10
sources.snippet: SK Hynix said it expects to start trading on July 10, but added that the dates were tentative and subject to change. Plans to issue 17.79 million new shares at 45.45 trillion won ($29.65 billion).

sources.title: Memory Chipmaker SK Hynix Kicks Off $28 Billion US Listing
sources.snippet: SK Hynix kicked off its formal marketing process for its US listing on Monday (July 6). Expects ADRs to begin trading July 10. Underwriters: BofA, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan.

sources.title: SK Hynix estimates net proceeds of $28B in massive Nasdaq ADR offering
sources.snippet: Net proceeds ~$28 billion; offering launched July 6; trading expected as early as July 10; 10 ADRs represent one common share.

sources.snippet: Amended registration statement for SK Hynix ADR offering.

sources.title: SK hynix Inc. - Form F-1 - FY2026
sources.snippet: Initial F-1 registration statement filed June 24, 2026.

sources.title: SK Hynix (SKHY) US$28bn Nasdaq Listing: Buy or Wait?
sources.snippet: Trading expected to begin Friday 10 July under ticker SKHY, once bookbuilding sets the final price. Largest US debut since SpaceX.

sources.title: SK Hynix to List on Nasdaq on July 10, Poised to Set Record for Overseas Company Listing in US
sources.snippet: SK Hynix will list on Nasdaq July 10, raising ~43 trillion won through ADR offering, second-largest global equity offering.

sources.title: SK Hynix Stock's Upcoming U.S. Listing Is a Sign the Memory Trade Could Soon Topple
sources.snippet: Debut tentatively scheduled for Friday July 10; would raise ~$29 billion, largest ADR listing in recorded history.

sources.title: SK Hynix plans $29 billion Nasdaq ADR listing in 2026
sources.snippet: Filed with SEC June 24; trading expected July 10; up to 17.79M new shares; each common share represented by ten ADRs.

sources.title: SK hynix (000660.KS) Stock Price History
sources.snippet: Seoul-listed shares closed 2,134,000 won on 2026-07-08, down 3.05%; 52-week high 2,987,000; down ~28.6% from high.
